revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=久米御縣神社| 所在地=奈良県橿原市久米町786| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=高皇産霊命
大来目命
天櫛根命| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=[[春日造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・大来目命（[[天久米命]]）・天櫛根命を祀る。 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いづれも皇室の御料地である[[御県]]（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 久米氏の衰退とともに当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建され、当社はその鎮守社として再建された。江戸時代中期頃からは「天満宮」「天神社」と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 明治初年の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamato/html/031332-01.html 久米御県神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/as/asuka/kume.htm 久米御縣神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=久米御縣神社| 所在地=奈良県橿原市久米町786| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=高皇産霊命
大来目命
天櫛根命| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=[[春日造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・大来目命（[[天久米命]]）・天櫛根命を祀る。 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いづれも皇室の御料地である[[御県]]（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 久米氏の衰退とともに当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建され、当社はその鎮守社として再建された。江戸時代中期頃からは「天満宮」「天神社」と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 明治初年の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamato/html/031332-01.html 久米御県神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/as/asuka/kume.htm 久米御縣神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:橿原市]]

revision.comment: /* 歴史 */ 式内社のカテゴリ変更。

{{神社| 名称=久米御縣神社| 所在地=奈良県橿原市久米町786| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=高皇産霊命
大来目命
天櫛根命| 社格=式内社（小）、村社| 本殿=[[春日造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・大来目命（[[天久米命]]）・天櫛根命を祀る。 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いずれも皇室の御料地である[[御県]]（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 久米氏の衰退とともに当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建され、当社はその鎮守社として再建された。江戸時代中期頃からは「天満宮」「天神社」と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 明治初年の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{神社| 名称=久米御縣神社| 画像=[[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]]| 所在地=奈良県橿原市久米町786| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=高皇産霊命
大来目命
天櫛根命| 社格=式内社（小）、村社| 本殿=[[春日造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=|}} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・大来目命（[[天久米命]]）・天櫛根命を祀る。 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いずれも皇室の御料地である[[御県]]（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 久米氏の衰退とともに当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建され、当社はその鎮守社として再建された。江戸時代中期頃からは「天満宮」「天神社」と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 明治初年の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 == 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:久米氏|社]]

{{神社| 名称=久米御縣神社| 画像=[[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]]| 所在地=奈良県橿原市久米町786| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=高皇産霊命
大来目命
天櫛根命| 社格=式内社（小）、村社| 本殿=[[春日造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]| 神事=|}} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・大来目命（[[天久米命]]）・天櫛根命を祀る。 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は[[久米部]]の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いずれも皇室の御料地である[[御県]]（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 久米氏の衰退とともに当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建され、当社はその鎮守社として再建された。江戸時代中期頃からは「天満宮」「天神社」と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 明治初年の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 == 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:久米氏|社]]

[[天久米命|大来目命]]
天櫛根命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、[[村社]] | 本殿=[[春日造]] | 例祭=10月14日 | 神事= }} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いずれも皇室の御料地である御県（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、式内社・小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 [[久米氏]]の衰退と共に当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建されると当社はその鎮守社・[[天神社]]または久米宮として再建された。[[江戸時代]]中期頃からは[[天満宮]]や天神社と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]、[[天久米命|大来目命]]、天櫛根命 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[久米部]]の[[祖神]]である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 == 境内 == *本殿 *[[皇大神社]] - 祭神：[[応神天皇|誉田別命]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] *[[熊野神社]] - 祭神：[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]] *拝殿 == 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:久米氏|社]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年8月}} {{神社 | 名称=久米御縣神社 | 画像=[[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]久米町786 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]
[[大久米命|大来目命]]
天櫛根命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、[[村社]] | 本殿=[[春日造]] | 例祭=10月14日 | 神事= }} '''久米御縣神社'''（くめのみあがたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]久米町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。「御県神社」という神社は[[大和国]]内に7社あり、いずれも皇室の御料地である御県（みあがた）に鎮座していたものである。『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]27年に「是歳、来目邑に屯倉を興す」という記述があり、創建もその頃とみられる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「大和国[[高市郡]] 久米御縣神社三座」と記載され、式内社・小社に列格している。ただし、延喜式祈年祝詞には他の6つの御県神社が記載されているが、当社は含まれていない。 [[久米氏]]の衰退と共に当社も衰微したが、隣接して[[久米寺]]が創建されると当社はその鎮守社・[[天神社]]または久米宮として再建された。[[江戸時代]]中期頃からは[[天満宮]]や天神社と呼ばれるようになった。現在も、「天満宮」「天神社」と書かれた燈籠が数多く残る。 [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）の[[神仏分離]]により久米寺から分離され、明治中期に久米御縣神社に改称した。また、[[村社]]に列せられている。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]、[[大来目命]]、天櫛根命 『五郡神社記』では「天櫛根大久米命」で一つの神名としており、『式内社調査報告』では、元は一柱の神であったものが近世に2つに分割して伝えられたものであると考えられるとしている。 大来目命は[[神武天皇]]に使えた[[久米部]]遠祖の人物である。一帯は久米部の居住地であった来目邑であり、その祖神を祀ったものである。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * [[皇大神社]] - 祭神：[[応神天皇|誉田別命]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] * [[熊野神社]] - 祭神：[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]] * 拝殿 == 関連項目 == * [[久米氏]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみあかたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:久米氏|社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|WikiCoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern Shrine Ranking System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} - Deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Kume Miyatake Shrine]] to [[Kume Miyanaka Shrine]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|WikiCoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern Shrine Ranking System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} - Deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. Tenkushine no Mikoto | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]][[Category:11]]

It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=draft}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]]

The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=draft}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|12=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|12=draft}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=draft}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|12=draft}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=draft}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]]

The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] [[Category:Kume Miyanaka Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]]

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Kume clan|Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]
