revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG|250px]] | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "prefecture = {{ill|" to "location = {{ill|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369609]] Wikidata name: Kume no Miagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=}} and became known as Tenjinja or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinja, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369609]] Wikidata name: Kume no Miagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]]

{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]]

It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369609]] Wikidata name: Kume no Miagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[sn:Kumenomiagata Shrine]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11369609]] Wikidata name: Kume no Miagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kumenomiagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumenomiagata Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kumenomiagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kumenomiagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenomiagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern typeing System]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kumenomiagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kumenomiagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}}

{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Kume no Miagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Miagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked shinto shrines]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kume no Miagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly referred to as [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In [[:en:1868|1868]] (first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}}

The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] }} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}}
