It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

revision.text:
[[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kume no Miagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kume no Miagata Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|WD=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Kume no Miagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Miagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked shinto shrines]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kume no Miagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

[[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kume no Miagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kume no Miagata Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|WD=Q55529547}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Kume no Miagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Miagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked shinto shrines]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kume no Miagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkumemikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|1=Kume-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkume no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kume no Miagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kume no Miagata Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkume no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|WD=Q55529547|1=Ōkume no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} '''Kume no Miagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Miagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked shinto shrines]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kume no Miagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|1=Kume-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkume no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

[[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kume no Miagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kume no Miagata Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, [[Kashihara City]], Kume Town 786 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.483056|N|135.79|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|34|28|59|N|135|47|24|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title | deities = {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125400}}
{{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkume no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529547}}
Tenkushine no Mikoto | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Village Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = October 14 | ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} | deity = {{ill|Q55529547|qid=Q55529547|1=Ōkume no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} '''Kume no Miagata Shrine''' ({{ill|Kume no Miagata Shrine|ja|久米御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Miagata Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kume Town, [[Kashihara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Village Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of ranked shinto shrines]]. == History == The exact origins of the shrine are unknown. Shrines named "Miagata Shrine" (御縣神社) exist in seven locations within the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, all of which were located on imperial estates called "Miagata" (御縣). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Suinin's 27th year contains a record stating, "This year, a granary was established in Kume Village." It is believed that the shrine was established during this period. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Yamato Province, Takichi District, Kume no Miagata Shrine, Three Seats" and is categorized as a minor {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. However, while the {{ill|Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja|延喜式祈年祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Kinen Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the other six Miagata Shrines, this shrine is excluded. The decline of the [[Kume clan]] led to the deterioration of the shrine. It was later reconstructed as a guardian shrine for the adjacent {{ill|Kumedera Temple|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumedera Temple|1=Kume-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369593}} and became known as Tenjinsha or Kumemiya. By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly referred to as {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} or Tenjinsha, with numerous lanterns bearing these names still preserved. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), during the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the shrine was detached from Kumedera Temple and renamed Kume no Miagata Shrine in the mid-Meiji period. It was also designated as a [[Village Shrine]]. == Deities == * Primary Deities: {{ill|Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125400}}, {{ill|Ōkumemikoto|ja|大来目命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkumemikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大久米命|1=Ōkume no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529547}}, and Tenkushine no Mikoto According to the ''Gokō-gun Shrine Records,'' the deity "Tenkushine no Ōkumemikoto" was initially treated as a single entity. The ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' suggests that the deity's name may have been divided into two during the early modern period. Ōkumemikoto served {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and is considered the ancestor of the [[Kume clan]]. The surrounding area, historically known as Kume Village, was home to the Kume clan, and the shrine enshrines their ancestral deity. == Structures == * Main Hall - {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} * {{ill|Kōdai Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}} - Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} - deity: {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} * Worship Hall == See Also == * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|久米御縣神社|version=84222063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久米御縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11369609}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
